sourceMovie.title: Insomnia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Insomnia works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jack Reacher
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Insomnia excels in thriller, Jack Reacher (2012) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Bone Collector
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Bone Collector (1999) is the natural next step after Insomnia: same thriller DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Kiss the Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Insomnia who respond to its thriller will find Kiss the Girls (1997) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Death Sentence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Insomnia fans keep landing on Death Sentence (2007) — the thriller is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Breach
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Breach draws from the same thriller well as Insomnia, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2007 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Gone Baby Gone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Gone Baby Gone (2007) captures the crime appeal of Insomnia without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts Gone Baby Gone at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Blue Velvet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of Insomnia — that dark edge, the crime — translates cleanly into Blue Velvet (1986). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Solace
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Solace (2015) clearly understood what makes thriller work — the same quality that keeps Insomnia rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Shoot 'Em Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shoot 'Em Up (2007) is the kind of film Insomnia fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The thriller overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Murder on the Orient Express
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Murder on the Orient Express shares Insomnia's gift for thriller — the 1974 film delivers drama without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Insomnia?
faq.answer: Try Jack Reacher, The Bone Collector, Kiss the Girls—each shares DNA with Insomnia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Insomnia?
faq.answer: Insomnia (2002) is best described as Thriller, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Insomnia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
